---
title: Births over time
format: html
---

```{r}
# Packages and data

library(tidyverse)
library(scales)
library(systemfonts)
library(ragg)
library(fable)
library(tsibble)
library(readxl)
library(ggtime)
library(feasts)
library(urca)

# dc_births <- read_csv("data/processed/dc-births-1995-2025.csv")

conflicts_prefer(dplyr::filter)

dc_births <- read_xlsx(
	"data/raw/EdScape_Chapter 1 Population and Students_Births_2026.xlsx",
	sheet = "Ch.1 Births Citywide",
	.name_repair = str_to_snake
)

dc_births <- dc_births |>
	select(
		year_code = year,
		births = number_of_births,
		population = total_population,
		birth_rate = birth_rate_per_1_000_population
	)

births_ts <- dc_births |>
	as_tsibble(index = year_code)

# # Helper to save plots as high-res PNGs with golden-ratio aspect ratio
# rlsave <- function(...) {
#   ggsave(
#     device = ragg::agg_png(),
#     width = 1000,
#     height = 1000 / ((1 + sqrt(5)) / 2),
#     scale = 2,
#     units = "px",
#     dpi = "retina",
#     ...
#   )
# }

# Custom ggplot theme built on theme_minimal
theme_rory <- theme_minimal(
	base_size = 12,
	base_family = "Lato-Regular",
	header_family = "PlayfairDisplayRoman-SemiBold",
	paper = "#fbf5f5",
	ink = "#070a0c",
	accent = "#b46466"
) +
	theme(
		plot.title.position = "plot",
		title = element_text(size = 14),
		axis.title = element_text(size = 12),
		axis.text = element_text(size = 10)
	)

set_theme(theme_rory)
```

```{r}
births_ts |>
	autoplot(births)
```

```{r}
# Compute y-axis limits: pad by 1/3 of the data range above and below,
# then round to nearest thousand for clean axis breaks
ymax <- max(dc_births$births)
ymin <- min(dc_births$births)
span <- ymax - ymin

yupper <- round(ymax + (1 / 3) * span, -3)
ylower <- round(ymin - (1 / 3) * span, -3)

births_plot <- dc_births |>
	ggplot(aes(x = as.integer(year_code), y = births, group = 1)) +
	geom_smooth(
		method = "loess",
		color = "#339589",
		fill = "#b8b5a8",
		lineend = "round"
	) +
	geom_line(
		linewidth = 1,
		color = "#335195",
		lineend = "round",
		linejoin = "round"
	) +
	labs(
		title = "Births in the District of Columbia (1995-2025)",
		x = "Year",
		y = "Births"
	) +
	scale_x_continuous(
		breaks = seq.int(1995, 2025, 5)
	) +
	scale_y_continuous(
		breaks = seq.int(ylower, yupper, 1000),
		limits = c(ylower, yupper),
		expand = expansion(c(0, 0)),
		labels = label_number(scale_cut = cut_short_scale())
	) +
	theme_sub_panel(
		grid.major.x = element_blank(),
		grid.minor = element_blank()
	)

# rlsave(
#   filename = "outputs/dc-births-95-25.png",
#   plot = births_plot
# )

births_plot
```

```{r}
births_ts |>
	gg_tsdisplay(births)
```

```{r}
births_ts |>
	gg_tsdisplay(difference(births))
```

```{r}
best_model <- births_ts |>
	model(
		arima210 = ARIMA(births ~ pdq(2, 1, 0)),
		stepwise = ARIMA(births),
		search = ARIMA(births, stepwise = FALSE)
	)
```

```{r}
best_model |>
	mutate(var = "Births") |>
	pivot_longer(
		cols = !var,
		names_to = "Model name",
		values_to = "Orders"
	)

glance(best_model) |> arrange(AICc) |> select(.model:BIC)
```

```{r}
best_model |>
	select(arima210) |>
	gg_tsresiduals()
```

```{r}
augment(best_model) |>
	filter(.model == "arima210") |>
	features(.innov, ljung_box, lag = 10, dof = 3)
```

```{r}
best_model |>
	forecast(h = 5) |>
	filter(.model == "arima210") |>
	autoplot(births_ts)
```

```{r}
final_model <- auto.arima(births_ts, c(1, 1, 0))
summary(final_model)
```

```{r}
final_forecast <- forecast(final_model, h = 5)
```

```{r}
autoplot(final_forecast)
```

```{r}
final_forecast
```
